notes: Entry-level design service targeting homeowners looking for single-room updates. Moderate CPL with good conversion potential for visual-forward campaigns.
source: Derived from home services advertising benchmarks and industry project value data (BusinessDojo 2025, BG Collective 2026)

notes: High-value full-home design projects. Longer sales cycle but excellent ROI due to large project values. Target affluent homeowners and new construction buyers.
source: Derived from remodeling CPL benchmarks ($150-400) adjusted for interior design; project values from ASID industry data

notes: Kitchen and bath design is a high-demand niche. Strong search intent from homeowners planning renovations. Pairs well with contractor referral partnerships.
source: Derived from kitchen/bath remodeling CPC benchmarks ($8-18 CPC, BG Collective 2026) and industry project values

notes: B2B-oriented service targeting office, retail, and hospitality spaces. Longer sales cycle with higher project values. LinkedIn may complement Google for this service.
source: Estimated from B2B/commercial service benchmarks; project values from Fortune Business Insights 2025

notes: Lower CPL due to less competition in staging niche. Target real estate agents and homeowners preparing to sell. Seasonal peaks during spring/summer selling season.
source: Lower CPL due to less competition; staging project values from industry reports

notes: Lower-value quick consultation service. Good entry point to upsell full design packages. Target homeowners doing DIY renovations who need professional guidance.
source: Lower-value quick consultation service; CPL estimated from general home services benchmarks

notes: Architecture firms compete in the B2B professional services space. Commercial projects (office buildings, corporate interiors, retail spaces) have long sales cycles but very high project values. CPL derived from LocaliQ 2025 Professional Services benchmarks ($103 CPL) adjusted upward for architecture's niche, high-value positioning.
source: LocaliQ 2025 Professional Services Benchmarks ($103.54 CPL); WordStream B2B benchmarks ($116.13 CPA). Adjusted for architecture industry positioning.

notes: Healthcare facility design (dental offices, medical practices, clinics) is a specialized niche with higher CPL due to narrow targeting. Very high project values justify the cost. Long RFP-based sales cycles typical.
source: LocaliQ 2025 Professional Services Benchmarks ($103.54 CPL) and Industrial & Commercial ($85.63 CPL). Adjusted upward for healthcare architecture specialization.

notes: Retail and hospitality architecture (restaurants, retail stores, hotels) has moderate competition. Project values are strong. Clients often search for architects with specific hospitality or retail portfolio experience.
source: LocaliQ 2025 Professional Services Benchmarks ($103.54 CPL); Industrial & Commercial ($85.63 CPL). Blended and adjusted for retail/hospitality architecture.

notes: Residential architecture (custom homes, additions, major renovations) has broader search volume but lower project values than commercial. Homeowners actively search for architects when planning significant projects.
source: LocaliQ 2025 Professional Services Benchmarks ($103.54 CPL); WordStream B2B benchmarks. Adjusted for residential architecture market dynamics.

notes: Building renovations and adaptive reuse (converting warehouses, historic buildings) is a growing niche. Lower CPL than new construction due to less keyword competition. Strong project values.
